data.title: Dow drops over 250 points amid US-Iran tensions, fear index remains high. Deere & Co. jumps 11.6%, Walmart falls 1.4%, Booking Holdings drops 6.2%. US trade deficit rises to $70.3 billion in December.
data.description: The CNN Money Fear and Greed index reported a rise in fear levels and remained in the "Fear" zone on Thursday. In the U.S. stock market, the Dow Jones index experienced a drop of over 250 points due to President Donald Trump's ambiguous stance on potential military action in Iran. Deere & Co. (NYSE: DE) saw an 11.6% increase after posting positive quarterly earnings, while Walmart Inc. (NASDAQ: WMT) declined by 1.4% despite meeting expectations. Booking Holdings Inc. (NASDAQ: BKNG) also fell by 6.2% following its fourth-quarter results. Economic data revealed an increase in the U.S. trade deficit to $70.3 billion in December, compared to $53 billion in the previous month. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Clarins launches AI Shade Finder for precise foundation matching, revolutionizing personalized makeup in stores.
data.description: In Paris on February 20, 2026, Clarins, known for its pioneering spirit since 1954, introduced its newest beauty tech innovation called the Clarins AI Shade Finder. This in-store service utilizes AI technology to redefine personalized makeup experiences for customers. The AI Shade Finder is an exclusive offering that aims to revolutionize the way individuals find their perfect makeup shades. The launch marks a significant step forward for Clarins in enhancing customer engagement and satisfaction.

data.title: Hankook's Winter i*Pike SR10W tires help ensure safe rally conclusion in extreme winter conditions at Rally Sweden.
data.description: The recently concluded Round 2 of Rally Sweden took place under challenging winter conditions, with the entirety of the route blanketed in snow and thick ice. Despite the harsh environment, Hankook's Winter i*Pike SR10W tires showcased remarkable performance, ensuring consistent driving stability for the competitors throughout the rally, including the severe icy stretches. Looking ahead, the next event to mark on the calendar is Round 3 of the 2026 World Rally Championship, which is scheduled to be held in Naivasha.

data.title: Amazon overtakes Walmart on Fortune 500 list after 13 years, with $716.9B revenue exceeding Walmart's $713.2B.
data.description: Amazon.com (NASDAQ: AMZN) has surpassed Walmart Inc. (NYSE: WMT) to claim the #1 spot on the Fortune 500 list after 13 years. In the previous fiscal year, Walmart generated total revenue of $713.2 billion, up by 4.7% from the year before. However, Amazon's full-year revenue of $716.9 billion, reported a fortnight ago, slightly edged past Walmart's revenue. The updated Fortune 500 list featuring Amazon on top is set to be revealed in early June, barring unforeseen circumstances. Amazon's ascent to the apex of the Fortune 500 list can be attributed to its strategic decisions, including discontinuing Amazon Fresh and Amazon Go in January 2026 to focus on more profitable endeavors such as Whole Foods, intensifying competition with not just Walmart but also Target. For the complete article, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Elizabeth Warren urges Trump Admin not to stabilize Bitcoin's price; Changpeng Zhao responds: "Crypto never needed a bailout."
data.description: The co-founder of Binance, Changpeng "CZ" Zhao, responded to Sen. Elizabeth Warren's concerns about government intervention in the current bear market. Senator Warren had asked the Trump administration to avoid bailing out billionaires. CZ disagreed with Warren's stance, stating that cryptocurrency, such as Bitcoin, does not require government rescues. He argued that any intervention would only benefit crypto billionaires. CZ emphasized that the cryptocurrency industry has never relied on bailouts and will not in the future. These comments came after Warren advised against stabilizing Bitcoin prices through government actions.

data.title: Mobilize Financial Services reports growth in 2025, excluding equity-acccounted companies, with a rise in Average Performing Assets.
data.description: Excluding equity-accounted companies, the Average Performing Assets (APA) were calculated as the average performing outstandings, which included the assets associated with operational leasing activities. This metric encompassed the average of performing outstandings at month-end for retail customers, and the average of daily performing outstandings for the dealer network.
